task.title: Permanently configuring a network device to accept all traffic using nmcli

context.p: You can use the nmcli utility to permanently configure a network device to accept all traffic regardless of the MAC addresses.

step.cmd: Optional: Display the network interfaces to identify the one for which you want to receive all traffic:

info.codeblock: # ip address show 1: enp1s0: <NO-CARRIER,BROADCAST,MULTICAST,UP> mtu 1500 qdisc fq_codel state DOWN group default qlen 1000 link/ether 98:fa:9b:a4:34:09 brd ff:ff:ff:ff:ff:ff ...
info.p: You can create a new connection, if you do not have any.

step.cmd: Modify the network device to enable or disable this property.

li.p: To enable the ethernet.accept-all-mac-addresses mode for enp1s0:
li.codeblock: # nmcli connection modify enp1s0 ethernet.accept-all-mac-addresses yes

li.p: To disable the accept-all-mac-addresses mode for enp1s0:
li.codeblock: # nmcli connection modify enp1s0 ethernet.accept-all-mac-addresses no

step.cmd: Apply the changes, reactivate the connection:

li.p: Verify that the ethernet.accept-all-mac-addresses mode is enabled:
The 802-3-ethernet.accept-all-mac-addresses: true indicates that the mode is enabled.
